findings.detail: Clean TD render, no overlaps or truncation. All five standard shapes present with correct semantics: Start/End ovals (terminator), rectangles (process), diamond (decision) with labelled Yes/No exits, parallelogram for 'Serve the tea' (output — matches corpus worked example exactly), arrows as flowlines. Loop back 'Wait 30 seconds' -> 'Water boiled?' is clearly visible as a circle on the page, mirroring the corpus Loops section. Top-to-bottom flow honours the corpus's own 'flow top-to-bottom' convention.

Soft. Subtitle reads 'process — loops back' while every other node uses the `role — shape-name` pattern ('process — rectangle', 'output — parallelogram'). In a notation-teaching diagram a student could parse 'loops back' as a shape name. Fix: change to 'process — rectangle' (the loop is already self-evident from the return arrow).

preflight.concept_fidelity: all labels trace to corpus Core Concepts / Implementation worked example
preflight.shape_semantics: oval=terminator OK, rectangle=process OK, diamond=decision OK, parallelogram=output OK — no wrong-shape findings
